name: The Landing at Fort Harrison Golf Course
about: The Landing at Fort Harrison Golf Course is a public golf facility located in Terre Haute, Indiana. Established in 1925 as a private club, the course is now accessible to all skill levels and features a par 72 layout spread over 60 acres, with options to play 9 or 18 holes. The venue is open year-round with indoor golf simulators available during the winter months. Pricing is straightforward, with no tee times required, offering walk-up play for 18 holes with a cart for $30, with special rates for seniors, students, and twilight play. The facility combines traditional outdoor play with modern indoor simulation technology to provide a comprehensive golfing experience regardless of the season.
why_golfers_like_it: No tee times required for flexible play
Affixed pricing with special rates for seniors and students
Indoor simulators available for year-round practice
Known for having the best greens and bunkers in the area

food_and_drink.notes: Food service available in the 19th Hole lounge

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The course provides carts for play, but it's recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: Food service is available in the 19th Hole lounge, but no specific BYOB policy is mentioned.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: No specific time is mentioned for 18 holes, but it's a traditional golf course play time.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is family-friendly and welcomes golfers of all skill levels, including juniors.
